events.title: US Strikes Indian-Crewed Tankers Off Oman
events.desc: The US military confirmed conducting a third strike on Indian-crewed tankers off the coast of Oman. A US jet fired missiles into the tanker Jalveer, while a previous strike on the Settebello resulted in the deaths of three Indian sailors. These attacks come amid heightened tensions in the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: US and Iran Exchange Cross-Border Attacks
events.desc: The United States and Iran engaged in reciprocal cross-border attacks, marking the second consecutive day of hostilities. Iran reported targeting American assets in Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain, with Bahrain's capital experiencing damage overnight. This escalation follows previous strikes and heightens regional instability.

events.title: Israeli Air Strikes Hit Lebanese City of Tyre
events.desc: Israeli air strikes targeted the Lebanese city of Tyre, despite a prior warning from Iran to cease attacks on its ally Hezbollah. The strikes occurred in response to Hezbollah's firing at Israeli territory, indicating a continued cycle of retaliatory actions between Israel and Hezbollah, with Iran involved.

events.title: Hezbollah Dead Buried in Temporary Graves in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Displaced families in southern Lebanon are resorting to temporary graves for Hezbollah fighters killed in Israeli strikes. This situation highlights the ongoing conflict and the inability of families to return to their villages due to the hostilities.

events.title: Iran-Born Engineer Granted Bail Ahead of Trial Tied to Drone Strike
events.desc: An Iranian-born engineer, Mahdi Sadeghi, has been released on bail days before his trial on charges related to the procurement of technology for Iran's military drones. The charges are linked to a deadly drone attack on a US military base in Jordan in 2024.

events.title: Trump Claims Imminent Deal with Iran, Tehran Cautious
events.desc: US President Donald Trump stated that a deal to end the Middle East war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz could be signed soon, potentially over the weekend. However, Iran's Foreign Ministry indicated that no final decision has been made and that they will not compromise on their 'red lines'.

events.title: Israeli Strikes in Southern Lebanon Kill 17
events.desc: Reports indicate that Israeli air strikes in southern Lebanon have resulted in 17 fatalities. Nine of these deaths occurred in a series of attacks in the town of Tayr Debba, according to Lebanon's state news agency, highlighting the severe impact of the ongoing conflict.

events.title: US Strikes Iran in Response to Helicopter Downing
events.desc: President Donald Trump announced that the US military conducted strikes against Iran in retaliation for the downing of a US military helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz. The US had previously accused Iran of shooting down the aircraft.

events.title: Israel Strikes Beirut Suburb Amidst Truce Concerns
events.desc: Israel launched strikes on a Beirut suburb, days after a US-brokered truce. The Israeli military stated the attacks were ordered in response to Hezbollah firing at Israeli territory, indicating a fragile ceasefire and continued cross-border exchanges.

events.title: US Plans to Deport Iranians to Central African Republic
events.desc: The Trump administration is reportedly planning to deport Iranians and other migrants to the Central African Republic. Concerns have been raised that some of these individuals, including a Christian convert and a pro-democracy activist, could face torture and persecution if returned to Iran.

events.title: Russia and Ukraine Exchange Cross-Border Attacks
events.desc: Cross-border attacks between Russia and Ukraine resulted in casualties on both sides. Two people were killed in Russia's Bryansk region, while a woman died in Ukraine's Sumy region, indicating a continuation of hostilities along the shared border.
